title: Building Scalable Laravel APIs with Docker & CI/CD Pipelines
excerpt: A deep dive into containerizing Laravel applications, setting up automated deployment pipelines, and achieving zero-downtime releases in production environments.
description: Modern web applications demand reliability and scalability. In this comprehensive guide, we explore how to containerize a Laravel application using Docker, configure multi-stage builds to minimize image size, and wire up a full CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions. We cover environment parity between dev and prod, secrets management, health checks, and rolling deployments — everything you need to ship with confidence.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: React 19 Deep Dive: Compiler, Actions & the New Hooks
excerpt: React 19 ships the long-awaited compiler, form Actions, and a suite of new hooks. Here's what changes for everyday development.
description: React 19 is the most significant release in years. The React Compiler eliminates manual memoization, Actions replace boilerplate form handlers, and new hooks like useOptimistic and useFormStatus make optimistic UI trivial. This article walks through each feature with practical before/after code examples and migration tips for existing codebases.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: PostgreSQL Performance Tuning: Indexes, EXPLAIN & Query Plans
excerpt: Slow queries are silent killers. Learn how to read EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, choose the right index type, and cut query time by 10×.
description: Database performance is often the bottleneck nobody talks about until it's too late. This guide covers the anatomy of a PostgreSQL query plan, how to interpret EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, when to use B-tree vs GIN vs BRIN indexes, partial indexes, covering indexes, and how connection pooling with PgBouncer can transform throughput under load.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: Micro-Frontend Architecture with Module Federation
excerpt: How to split a monolithic React app into independently deployable micro-frontends using Webpack 5 Module Federation.
description: As frontend codebases grow, monolithic SPAs become hard to scale across teams. Module Federation lets you share live code between separately deployed apps at runtime. We build a host shell and two remote micro-frontends, share a design system, handle versioning conflicts, and discuss the trade-offs vs. monorepos.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: AWS Lambda Cold Starts: Causes, Costs & Cures
excerpt: Cold starts can add hundreds of milliseconds to serverless response times. Here's a systematic approach to diagnosing and eliminating them.
description: Serverless is fast — until it isn't. Cold starts occur when Lambda must initialize a new execution environment, and they can devastate user experience for latency-sensitive workloads. We examine the lifecycle of a Lambda invocation, measure cold start impact with real traces, and apply proven mitigations: provisioned concurrency, SnapStart for Java, lightweight runtimes, and connection reuse patterns.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: Designing REST APIs That Don't Embarrass You
excerpt: Versioning, error shapes, pagination, and the naming conventions that separate amateur APIs from ones developers actually enjoy using.
description: A well-designed API is a product. This article covers the principles behind resource naming, HTTP verb semantics, consistent error response envelopes, cursor-based pagination for large datasets, API versioning strategies (URI vs. header), rate limiting headers, and how to write OpenAPI specs that serve as living documentation rather than afterthoughts.
author: Shishir Ahmed

title: Tailwind CSS v4: What's New and Should You Upgrade?
excerpt: Tailwind v4 rewrites the engine in Rust, drops the config file, and introduces CSS-first configuration. A practical upgrade guide.
description: Tailwind CSS v4 is a ground-up rewrite powered by the Oxide engine (Rust + Lightning CSS). The config file is gone — configuration lives in CSS. The new @theme directive, automatic content detection, and composable variants change how you work day-to-day. We compare v3 vs v4 patterns, walk through a real migration, and benchmark build times.
author: Shishir Ahmed
